metadata.description: IMO topics with prerequisite ordering, colored by branch

nodes.label: AM-GM Inequality
nodes.description: Arithmetic Mean–Geometric Mean inequality: for non-negative reals, AM >= GM. Weighted AM-GM, applications to optimization and bounding expressions.

nodes.label: Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality
nodes.description: Cauchy-Schwarz inequality in Engel (Titu) form and standard form. Applications to bounding sums and proving inequalities.

nodes.label: Power Mean Inequality
nodes.description: Generalized mean inequality: M_r <= M_s for r <= s. QM-AM-GM-HM chain. Applications to homogeneous inequalities.

nodes.label: Rearrangement & Chebyshev
nodes.description: Rearrangement inequality: sum is maximized when sequences are similarly sorted. Chebyshev's sum inequality as corollary.

nodes.label: Schur's Inequality
nodes.description: Schur's inequality and its generalized forms. Applications to symmetric inequalities in three or more variables.

nodes.label: SOS (Sum of Squares)
nodes.description: Sum of Squares decomposition method for proving inequalities. Systematic approach to writing expressions as sums of squares.

nodes.label: Jensen's Inequality
nodes.description: Jensen's inequality for convex and concave functions. Applications to inequalities involving trigonometric, logarithmic, and power functions.

nodes.label: Tangent Line Trick
nodes.description: Using supporting lines of convex/concave functions to establish inequalities. Combining with Jensen's and smoothing/mixing variables.

nodes.label: Cauchy Functional Equation
nodes.description: Cauchy's functional equation f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) and its variants. Continuity conditions, pathological solutions, standard substitutions.

nodes.label: Functional Equation Techniques
nodes.description: Injectivity/surjectivity arguments, substitution strategies, fixed points, monotonicity. Solving competition-style functional equations.

nodes.label: Advanced Functional Equations
nodes.description: Functional equations over integers, functional inequalities, equations with additional constraints. IMO P6-level functional equations.

nodes.label: Polynomials & Roots
nodes.description: Factor theorem, root-finding, irreducibility criteria (Eisenstein, rational root). Polynomial division and remainder theorem.

nodes.label: Vieta's Formulas
nodes.description: Relations between roots and coefficients. Vieta jumping technique. Newton's sums connecting power sums and symmetric polynomials.

nodes.label: Symmetric Polynomials
nodes.description: Elementary symmetric polynomials, Newton's identities. Fundamental theorem of symmetric polynomials. Applications to competition problems.

nodes.label: Sequences & Recurrences
nodes.description: Linear recurrence relations, characteristic equation method. Nonlinear recurrences, periodicity, boundedness arguments.

nodes.label: Telescoping & Summation
nodes.description: Telescoping sums and products. Abel summation. Partial fractions for series evaluation.

nodes.label: Generating Functions (Intro)
nodes.description: Ordinary and exponential generating functions. Using generating functions to solve recurrences and counting problems at olympiad level.

nodes.label: Divisibility & GCD/LCM
nodes.description: Divisibility rules, GCD, LCM, Euclidean algorithm. Bezout's identity, properties of divisibility in the integers.

nodes.label: Primes & Factorization
nodes.description: Prime numbers, Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic. Infinitude of primes, prime factorization techniques, properties of primes.

nodes.label: Modular Arithmetic
nodes.description: Congruences, modular inverses, solving linear congruences. Residue systems and properties of congruence arithmetic.

nodes.label: Chinese Remainder Theorem
nodes.description: Chinese Remainder Theorem: existence and uniqueness of solutions to systems of congruences with coprime moduli. Constructive algorithms.

nodes.label: Fermat's Little Theorem
nodes.description: Fermat's Little Theorem: a^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p) for gcd(a,p)=1. Applications to computing powers and modular inverses.

nodes.label: Euler's Theorem & Totient
nodes.description: Euler's totient function phi(n), Euler's theorem a^phi(n) ≡ 1 (mod n). Multiplicativity of phi, formula for phi(n).

nodes.label: Order & Primitive Roots
nodes.description: Multiplicative order of an element mod n. Primitive roots: existence, counting, and applications. Index calculus.

nodes.label: Quadratic Residues
nodes.description: Quadratic residues mod p, Legendre symbol. Euler's criterion. Quadratic reciprocity law and its applications.

nodes.label: Linear Diophantine Equations
nodes.description: Linear Diophantine equations ax + by = c. Existence conditions via gcd, parametric solutions. Systems of linear Diophantine equations.

nodes.label: Pythagorean & Sum-of-Squares
nodes.description: Pythagorean triples parametrization. Sums of two and four squares theorems. Representing integers as sums of squares.

nodes.label: Pell's Equation
nodes.description: Pell's equation x^2 - Dy^2 = 1. Continued fraction method, fundamental solutions, recurrence for all solutions.

nodes.label: Advanced Diophantine Equations
nodes.description: Infinite descent, Vieta jumping on Diophantine equations. Nonlinear Diophantine equations, Fermat-type problems, modular obstructions.

nodes.label: p-adic Valuations
nodes.description: p-adic (v_p) valuation of integers. Properties: v_p(ab) = v_p(a)+v_p(b), v_p(a+b) >= min. Legendre's formula for v_p(n!).

nodes.label: Lifting the Exponent Lemma
nodes.description: LTE lemma: computing v_p(a^n - b^n) and v_p(a^n + b^n). Applications to divisibility problems in competition number theory.

nodes.label: Arithmetic Functions
nodes.description: Multiplicative functions: sigma, tau, phi, Mobius mu. Mobius inversion formula. Dirichlet convolution basics.

nodes.label: Number Bases & Digit Arguments
nodes.description: Representations in different bases, digit sum properties, base-dependent divisibility. Digital root arguments.

nodes.label: Counting Principles
nodes.description: Addition and multiplication principles. Permutations, combinations, multisets. Inclusion-exclusion principle.

nodes.label: Binomial Coefficients
nodes.description: Binomial theorem, Pascal's identity, Vandermonde's identity. Hockey stick identity, binomial coefficient identities and their proofs.

nodes.label: Double Counting
nodes.description: Counting the same quantity in two ways to derive identities or bounds. Handshake lemma. Combinatorial proofs.

nodes.label: Bijections & Correspondences
nodes.description: Establishing bijections to prove equinumerosity. Involutions, sign-reversing involutions, canonical bijections in combinatorics.

nodes.label: Pigeonhole Principle
nodes.description: Basic and generalized pigeonhole principle. Applications: Erdos-Szekeres theorem, sequences, covering arguments, Dirichlet's approximation.

nodes.label: Advanced Pigeonhole Applications
nodes.description: Pigeonhole in geometry (lattice points, convex sets), pigeonhole with modular arithmetic, infinite pigeonhole (Ramsey-flavored).

nodes.label: Combinatorial Identities
nodes.description: Proving and applying identities: Vandermonde, Chu-Vandermonde, Catalan numbers, Stirling numbers. Combinatorial vs algebraic proofs.

nodes.label: Recurrence Relations
nodes.description: Setting up and solving recurrences for counting problems. Characteristic equation method, generating function approach for linear recurrences.

nodes.label: Graph Theory Basics
nodes.description: Graphs, trees, bipartite graphs, connectivity. Handshaking lemma, Eulerian/Hamiltonian paths and cycles. Degree sequences.

nodes.label: Graph Coloring
nodes.description: Vertex and edge coloring, chromatic number. Greedy coloring, Brooks' theorem. Coloring arguments in competition problems.

nodes.label: Ramsey Theory
nodes.description: Ramsey numbers R(s,t), Ramsey's theorem. Party problem, Schur's theorem. Bounds on Ramsey numbers, Hales-Jewett type results.

nodes.label: Extremal Combinatorics
nodes.description: Turan's theorem, extremal set theory (Sperner, Dilworth). Kruskal-Katona theorem. Extremal problems on graphs and hypergraphs.

nodes.label: Invariants
nodes.description: Identifying quantities preserved under operations to prove impossibility or determine outcomes. Parity, coloring, modular invariants.

nodes.label: Monovariants
nodes.description: Quantities that change monotonically under operations. Proving termination of processes, bounding number of steps. Energy/potential arguments.

nodes.label: Probabilistic Method
nodes.description: Existence proofs via probability: expectation argument, first moment method, alteration. Lovász Local Lemma basics.

nodes.label: Combinatorial Geometry
nodes.description: Convex hull, lattice points (Pick's theorem), tiling problems. Helly's theorem, ham sandwich theorem, geometric Ramsey-type results.

nodes.label: Combinatorial Games
nodes.description: Nim and Sprague-Grundy theory. Winning/losing positions, game strategy. Symmetric strategies, strategy stealing.

nodes.label: Algorithms & Processes
nodes.description: Greedy algorithms, exchange arguments. Analyzing iterative processes: termination, eventual periodicity, limiting behavior.

nodes.label: Angle Chasing
nodes.description: Systematic angle chasing in geometric configurations. Inscribed angle theorem, angles in circles, angle bisector properties.

nodes.label: Similarity & Congruence
nodes.description: Triangle congruence (SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS) and similarity criteria. Ratio and proportion in similar figures.

nodes.label: Triangle Centers
nodes.description: Incenter, circumcenter, orthocenter, centroid. Nine-point circle, Euler line. Excenters and excircles. Properties and relations.

nodes.label: Ceva's & Menelaus' Theorems
nodes.description: Ceva's theorem for concurrent cevians. Menelaus' theorem for collinear points on triangle sides. Trigonometric forms.

nodes.label: Cyclic Quadrilaterals
nodes.description: Properties of cyclic quadrilaterals: opposite angles supplement, Ptolemy's theorem. Characterizations and applications.

nodes.label: Power of a Point
nodes.description: Power of a point with respect to a circle. Intersecting chords, secant-secant, secant-tangent relations. Signed power.

nodes.label: Radical Axes & Radical Center
nodes.description: Radical axis of two circles: locus of equal power. Radical center of three circles. Applications to concyclicity and collinearity.

nodes.label: Homothety
nodes.description: Homotheties (dilations) centered at a point. Homothety mapping circles to circles, finding tangent circles. Composition of homotheties.

nodes.label: Spiral Similarities
nodes.description: Spiral similarities (rotation + dilation). Identifying spiral centers, composition. Applications to proving concyclicity and collinearity.

nodes.label: Inversive Geometry
nodes.description: Circle inversion: mapping circles and lines, preserving angles. Choosing inversion center/radius strategically. Solving hard problems via inversion.

nodes.label: Projective Geometry
nodes.description: Cross-ratio and its invariance under projection. Harmonic conjugates and harmonic bundles. Poles and polars with respect to conics.

nodes.label: Trigonometric Cevians
nodes.description: Trigonometric form of Ceva's theorem. Stewart's theorem. Using trigonometry to compute lengths, angles, and areas in triangle configurations.

nodes.label: Trigonometric Identities in Geometry
nodes.description: Law of sines, law of cosines. Product-to-sum and sum-to-product formulas. Applying trig identities to prove geometric results.

nodes.label: Area Methods
nodes.description: Using signed areas and area ratios to prove collinearity, concurrency, and length relations. Shoelace formula, cross product areas.

nodes.label: Barycentric Coordinates
nodes.description: Barycentric coordinate system for triangles. Representing points, lines, circles. Distance formula, equation of circumcircle. Computational approach.

nodes.label: Complex Numbers in Geometry
nodes.description: Representing points as complex numbers. Rotation, reflection, translation via complex arithmetic. Unit circle technique for cyclic polygons.

nodes.label: Cartesian Coordinates
nodes.description: Analytic geometry approach: setting up coordinate systems strategically. Distance, midpoint, line equations. When to use coordinates vs synthetic.

nodes.label: Simson Line & Pedal Triangles
nodes.description: Simson line (pedal line) of a point on circumcircle. Pedal triangles, anti-pedal triangles. Applications to collinearity problems.

nodes.label: Isogonal & Isotomic Conjugates
nodes.description: Isogonal conjugate points, isotomic conjugate points. Symmedian point as isogonal conjugate of centroid. Applications to locus problems.

nodes.label: Tangent Lines & Tangent Circles
nodes.description: Tangent lines to circles, tangent length. Internally/externally tangent circles. Mixtilinear incircles. Monge's theorem.
